behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename semantics plus CLI parsing/output. A pure in-process mock can miss rename detection edge cases (thresholds, status formats), so mocking could hide real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a baseline file, rename it via git, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 added/0 deleted for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling depends on real git diff/output behavior and process I/O/encoding boundaries. Mocked diff text can easily miss true binary markers and crash paths.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary change is included in the correct category/count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git/CLI boundary case. In-process mocks often normalize this path and hide actual command failures from git in empty repos.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit (or expected non-error behavior) and an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history and git change classification. Mocking git history can pass while real command output/order/status parsing fails.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing added, deleted, renamed, and modified files across multiple commits; run the CLI and compare reported counts to independently computed ground truth from git commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real index vs working-tree semantics in git. Pure mocks can hide differences in staged/unstaged command paths and status resolution.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, produce the same file changes once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output (or normalized-equivalent if ordering is defined).
